name: Clarifi
description_short: Clarifi is a Philadelphia-area non-profit providing free financial counseling, housing assistance, and benefits access to low- and moderate-income residents across Southeastern PA, NJ, and DE.
description_long:
Clarifi was founded over 50 years ago and has established itself as Greater Philadelphia's largest provider of financial and housing empowerment services. The organization serves low- and moderate-income residents across Southeastern Pennsylvania, New Jersey, and Delaware with a mission centered on making judgment-free financial resources accessible to everyone regardless of income, zip code, or background.

The organization offers certified financial empowerment counseling, housing success programs, benefits access assistance, and direct assistance services. In 2024 alone, Clarifi conducted 4,911 counseling sessions serving 3,657 households, achieved 5,100+ client milestones, and disbursed $1.3 million in grants. Notably, they secured $6.9 million for essential home repairs and helped clients reduce average debt by $2,978. Their services are explicitly positioned as judgment-free and tailored to unique client situations and goals.

Clarifi distinguishes itself through its comprehensive approach combining financial counseling with direct assistance and tangible outcomes. The organization tracks and publicizes specific impact metrics, demonstrating measurable results in debt reduction, home repairs, and grant disbursement. They serve over 10,000 people annually through a combination of financial education, counseling, and supportive services. The organization invites community engagement through donations and volunteer opportunities, positioning itself as a community-integrated resource.

The main limitation is geographic scope—services are restricted to Southeastern Pennsylvania, New Jersey, and Delaware. As a non-profit relying on grants and donations, service availability may depend on funding cycles. The website provides limited details on specific counseling methodologies, wait times, or eligibility requirements beyond the general income-based focus.

When evaluating debt relief companies, consumers should compare settlement programs against alternatives like debt consolidation loans, which combine multiple debts into a single fixed-rate payment. Credit counseling through nonprofit agencies offers free budgeting help without impacting credit scores. For those whose credit has already been damaged, credit repair services can address inaccurate negative items on reports. Personal loans for bad credit may provide funds for debt payoff at lower rates than credit cards, and credit monitoring services help track progress throughout the recovery process. Nonprofit counselors can help consumers evaluate whether an installment loan for debt consolidation makes sense given their income and existing obligations.

pricing.guarantee_details: Contact provider for current pricing and guarantee details.

pros: Certified financial empowerment counseling provided by trained professionals
Secured $6.9 million for essential home repairs for clients in 2024
Completely judgment-free approach explicitly stated in mission
Direct financial assistance available ($1.3 million in grants disbursed in 2024)
Comprehensive services including housing success, benefits access, and debt counseling
Serving 10,000+ people annually across three-state region
Established 50+ year track record with measurable documented outcomes
Non-profit structure with no profit motive or predatory lending concerns
cons: Geographic limitation: only serves Southeastern PA, New Jersey, and Delaware
Website provides minimal information on wait times, appointment availability, or specific eligibility thresholds
Limited detail on counseling methodologies, certifications held by counselors, or service response times
Funding-dependent model may affect service availability during grant cycles
No indication of virtual/remote counseling options for clients unable to travel
best_for: Low- and moderate-income families seeking judgment-free debt and budget counseling in the PA/NJ/DE region
Homeowners needing assistance with essential repairs and accessing repair grant programs
Individuals seeking to build financial resilience and long-term wealth-building strategies
Residents seeking benefits access assistance and comprehensive financial empowerment support
diagnosis: Clarifi is best for low- to moderate-income residents in Southeastern PA, NJ, or Delaware seeking comprehensive, judgment-free financial counseling with direct assistance options. The primary caveat is strict geographic limitation—this organization cannot serve consumers outside its three-state service area.
typical_results_timeline: Results vary by individual situation. Contact the provider to discuss expected timelines for your specific needs.
meta_description: Philadelphia non-profit providing free financial counseling, housing assistance, and benefits to low- and moderate-income residents in PA, NJ, and DE.
